title: Sandboxed Agent Run Loop
summary: Runs agent work in an isolated workspace with explicit permissions, checkpoints, and review gates.
steps: Create an isolated workspace and declare allowed file, network, shell, and credential access before starting.
Have the agent plan within the permission manifest and escalate anything outside it.
Run bounded implementation steps with checkpoints that can be diffed and rolled back.
Use an independent review pass to verify tests, security, and completion evidence.
Ship only when permissions were respected and the review gate passes.
author: Palette Lab
prompt:
Use the Sandboxed Agent Run Loop for [task, workspace_policy, permission_manifest].

First define the task boundary, success criteria, allowed actions, required evidence, risk level, and budget. Work in bounded iterations. In each iteration:
- Create an isolated workspace and declare allowed file, network, shell, and credential access before starting.
- Have the agent plan within the permission manifest and escalate anything outside it.
- Run bounded implementation steps with checkpoints that can be diffed and rolled back.
- Use an independent review pass to verify tests, security, and completion evidence.
- Ship only when permissions were respected and the review gate passes.

Quality gates before completion:
- permission manifest exists
- no out-of-scope access used
- checkpoint diffs reviewed
- independent review passes

Stop when one of these is true:
- review gate passes
- permission escalation required
- sandbox cannot represent task safely

Finish with:
- permission manifest
- checkpoint list
- review findings
- test output

Escalate for human approval before: granting secrets, network access to private systems, deploying changes.
